prompt: Build a 12-month revenue model for a SaaS product with three tiers: Starter $29/mo, Pro $99/mo, Enterprise $299/mo. Assume 100 initial customers with 10% MoM growth and 5% monthly churn. Show MRR and ARR projections.

mock_result.response:
12-Month SaaS Revenue Model (Base Tier Distribution: 60% Starter, 30% Pro, 10% Enterprise)

Month 1: 100 customers — MRR $5,630 — ARR $67,560
Month 3: 115 customers — MRR $6,475 — ARR $77,700
Month 6: 145 customers — MRR $8,164 — ARR $97,968
Month 12: 195 customers — MRR $10,979 — ARR $131,748

Key Metrics:
- LTV (blended): $1,126
- Churn impact at 5%/mo: ~18 customers lost by M12 vs. 95 gross new
- Net Growth: +95 customers over 12 months
- Cumulative Revenue: ~$96,000

Notes: Enterprise tier (10%) contributes 53% of MRR despite smallest segment. Reducing churn to 3% increases Year 1 revenue by 22%.
